data.body: Potts (2025) argues this directly: AI regulation keeps failing because it uses tools built for ordinary objects on a hyperobject. Traditional governance assumes bounded, locatable things. AI resists this at every level.
data.author: did:plc:4j7exarb62djxycrgdfhuulr

data.body: Morton proposes hyposubjects instead. Distributed, humble, plural. 'Less than the sum of their parts.' Subscendent, not transcendent. 'The time of hypersubjects is ending because of the hyperobjective conditions they've created.'
